title: Leprosy Reactions Management - Training Data

type1_reaction.name: Type 1 Reaction (Reversal Reaction)
timing: Usually within 1 year after treatment completion

acute: Edema and color change of lesions
neuritis_signs: Weakness in muscle groups supplied by affected nerve
emergency_signs: Severe eye inflammation with vision changes

diagnosis.clinical: Based on history and examination findings
diagnosis.no_slit_skin_smear_change: May still show same or similar bacillary index

corticosteroids.monitoring: Weekly initially, then monthly for efficacy and side effects

treatment.duration: Continue MDT during treatment of reaction

type1_reaction.action_required: URGENT - Contact doctor immediately if suspected

type2_reaction.name: Type 2 Reaction (Erythema Nodosum Leprosum - ENL)
timing: Can occur up to 10 years after treatment completion
type2_reaction.risk_groups: More common in BL and LL patients (10-50% occurrence)
type2_reaction.frequency: Up to 50% of BL and LL patients

thalidomide.monitoring: Strict pregnancy prevention program (REMS in USA)

outcome: Can occur even after treatment completion
monitoring: Clinical assessment at each MDT visit

assessment: Detailed history of symptom onset and timeline

diagnosis.type2: Nodules + fever + systemic symptoms during treatment

reaction_management_flow.monitoring_duration: Months to years depending on severity

seek_immediate_medical_attention: Severe eye inflammation with vision loss or inability to close eye
Inability to move fingers or toes (paralysis)
Severe systemic symptoms (high fever, jaundice, confusion)

prevention_tips: Consistent MDT adherence reduces complication risk
Regular doctor visits aid early detection
Follow-up care is essential even after treatment
